Part B.key_evidence: The cutouts show strong artifact-like morphology: the Template panel has a hard black/white edge (bad-pixel mask or chip edge), and the Difference (Image) panel shows coherent wavy/striping patterns rather than a compact PSF-like residual at the center. FWHM is large (5.4 px), classtar is very low (0.116), and distpsnr1 = 14.1" means the nearest PS1 source is not a real host association. ncovhist is very high (2185) while ndethist = 16 is modest, and deltajd = 0 indicates this is effectively a first-epoch record.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: Leading interpretation is a bogus/artifact detection. The Template shows a masked region/edge; the Difference image is dominated by correlated wave-like residuals typical of bad subtractions near masked pixels or detector defects, not an isolated point source. The faint measured magpsf with large sigmapsf (0.22) is consistent with a low-SNR spurious detection.
Part B.alternative_analysis: A weak alternative is a genuine faint variable source in a poorly sampled region, but the central pixel does not show a clean PSF-like positive residual and the reference image is effectively masked at this location, making photometric subtraction unreliable. No PS1 host is co-located (distpsnr1 ~14"), so neither AGN nor SN host association is supported.
